sub: Technology block (a): describing every interface in the UTM diagram

title: What Is OpenAPI?
desc: A specification for describing RESTful APIs — machine-readable, human-friendly

items: OpenAPI is a specification for describing RESTful APIs
Enables a standardized definition of API endpoints and their functions
Originally developed as Swagger, now governed under the Linux Foundation (OpenAPI Initiative)
Standardization — uniform API documentation across teams and vendors
Automatic generation — code, documentation and test cases generated from the spec
Interactive use — try APIs live, e.g. in Swagger UI
Easier API design — collaboration between developers and stakeholders on one artifact
Remember Module 5: a NOTAM service, a METAR service, a UTM clearance service — all of them need exactly this kind of contract

title: Anatomy of an OpenAPI Document
desc: APIs are described in YAML or JSON — five top-level building blocks

body.code: openapi: 3.1.0 info: title: UTM Flight Clearance API version: 1.0.0 servers: - url: https://utm.example.com/v1 paths: /drones/{droneId}/clearance: post: summary: Request flight clearance for a mission responses: '201': { description: Clearance granted } '409': { description: Conflict with another mission }

items: openapi — version of the specification, info — metadata: title, version, description
servers — available servers, paths — definition of the API endpoints
components — reusable schema definitions (e.g. a Mission object shared by several endpoints)

body.alt: Comparison of OpenAPI 3.0 and 3.1 components sections

items: 3.1 aligns the schema language with full JSON Schema — no more OpenAPI-specific dialect
Practical advice: start new projects on 3.1; most generators support it today

title: Tooling Around OpenAPI
desc: Live demo — and the takeaway: standards lead to better collaboration

items: Swagger UI — visual rendering and in-browser test calls (try the live editor)
OpenAPI Generator — client/server code generation for dozens of languages

title: Swagger Editor: Spec Left, Docs Right
desc: The YAML you write and the documentation it generates — live in the browser

body.alt: Swagger Editor with the Petstore example: YAML on the left, rendered documentation with GET/POST endpoints on the right
body.caption: Try it yourself at editor.swagger.io — paste the Petstore example or your own UTM clearance API.
